records.ety: [F., fr. L. imperium a command, sovereignty, dominion, empire, fr. imperare. See Emperor; cf. Imperial.]

quotes: Over hell extend His empire, and with iron scepter rule.

defs: The dominion of an emperor; the territory or countries under the jurisdiction and dominion of an emperor (rarely of a king), usually of greater extent than a kingdom, always comprising a variety in the nationality of, or the forms of administration in, constituent and subordinate portions; as, the Austrian empire.
quotes: Empire carries with it the idea of a vast and complicated government.

defs: Any dominion; supreme control; governing influence; rule; sway; as, the empire of mind or of reason.
quotes: Another force which, in the Middle Ages, shared with chivalry the empire over the minds of men.
